  1. Movement analysis
  2. Home ranges

Home ranges

Introduction

Objectives:

  • Autocorrelated home range estimation
  • https://ecoisilva.github.io/AKDE_minireview/code/AKDE_R-tutorial.html
library(tidyverse)
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
✔ purrr     1.0.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

Attaching package: 'kableExtra'

The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':

    group_rows
library(ctmm)

Attaching package: 'ctmm'

The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':

    annotate

Step 1 - Formatting and loading an animal tracking dataset

c1 <- read.csv("../data/yt/gps.csv")
head(c1)
            timestamp longitude latitude    ID      season
1 2020-11-06 19:10:39 -129.2876 60.24530 43163 earlywinter
2 2020-11-07 01:07:00 -129.3327 60.19127 43163 earlywinter
3 2020-11-07 07:05:54 -129.3022 60.17469 43163 earlywinter
4 2020-11-07 13:04:39 -129.3021 60.17254 43163 earlywinter
5 2020-11-07 19:03:23 -129.3146 60.14960 43163 earlywinter
6 2020-11-08 01:02:25 -129.3380 60.14822 43163 earlywinter
glimpse(c1)
Rows: 90,864
Columns: 5
$ timestamp <chr> "2020-11-06 19:10:39", "2020-11-07 01:07:00", "2020-11-07 07…
$ longitude <dbl> -129.2876, -129.3327, -129.3022, -129.3021, -129.3146, -129.…
$ latitude  <dbl> 60.24530, 60.19127, 60.17469, 60.17254, 60.14960, 60.14822, …
$ ID        <int> 43163, 43163, 43163, 43163, 43163, 43163, 43163, 43163, 4316…
$ season    <chr> "earlywinter", "earlywinter", "earlywinter", "earlywinter", …

Convert to telemetry object:

c1t <- as.telemetry(c1)[['43140']]
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43140
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43141
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43142
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43143
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43144
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43145
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43146
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43147
Minimum sampling interval of 5.95 hours in 43148
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43149
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43150
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43151
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43152
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43153
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43154
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43155
Minimum sampling interval of 5.95 hours in 43156
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43157
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43158
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43159
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43160
Minimum sampling interval of 28.4 minutes in 43161
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43162
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43163
Minimum sampling interval of 5.94 hours in 43164
plot(c1t)
DOP values missing. Assuming DOP=1.

Step 2 - Check for the range residency assumption

level <- 0.95 # we want to display 95% confidence intervals
xlim <- c(0,2 %#% "month") # to create a window of 2 months
SVF <- variogram(c1t)
par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(SVF, fraction = 1, level = level)
abline(v = 1, col = "red", lty = 2) # adding a line at 1 month 
plot(SVF, xlim = xlim, level = level)
abline(v = 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

Step 3 - Select best-fit movement model through model selection

Calculate an automated model guesstimate

GUESS1 <- ctmm.guess(c1t, interactive = FALSE)
# Automated model selection, starting from GUESS
FIT1_ML <- ctmm.select(c1t, GUESS1, method = 'ML')
FIT1_pHREML <- ctmm.select(c1t, GUESS1, method = 'pHREML')
# Reminder: it will default to pHREML if no method is specified.
summary(FIT1_ML)
$name
[1] "OUF anisotropic"

$DOF
       mean        area   diffusion       speed 
   4.826251    9.343750 1536.327623 3160.767593 

$CI
                                          low          est         high
area (square kilometers)          8244.194197 17719.851857 30759.228166
τ[position] (months)                 2.514979     5.413527    11.652687
τ[velocity] (hours)                  2.646270     2.810199     2.984284
speed (kilometers/day)              10.150593    10.330684    10.510731
diffusion (square kilometers/day)   11.815615    12.429440    13.058591
summary(FIT1_pHREML)
$name
[1] "OUF anisotropic"

$DOF
       mean        area   diffusion       speed 
   4.210549    6.247827 1533.577869 3160.141773 

$CI
                                          low          est         high
area (square kilometers)          7945.432902 21116.696350 40612.445514
τ[position] (months)                 2.424391     6.452467    17.173107
τ[velocity] (hours)                  2.645376     2.809241     2.983256
speed (kilometers/day)              10.151094    10.331212    10.511287
diffusion (square kilometers/day)   11.821063    12.435743    13.065784

Summarize model

FIT1_pHREML_verbose <- ctmm.select(c1t, GUESS1, verbose = TRUE)
summary(FIT1_pHREML_verbose)
                    ΔAICc ΔRMSPE (km)  DOF[area]
OUF anisotropic    0.0000    18.48885   6.247804
OUF              561.8882    25.52312   5.525294
OU anisotropic  1063.5229    20.16934   4.213728
OUf anisotropic 4389.5706     0.00000 358.324029

Step 4 - Feeding a movement model into the home range estimator

Run an area-corrected AKDE:

UD1_ML <- akde(c1t, FIT1_ML)
Default grid size of 2.99166666666667 hours chosen for bandwidth(...,fast=TRUE).
UD1_pHREML <- akde(c1t, FIT1_pHREML)
Default grid size of 2.99166666666667 hours chosen for bandwidth(...,fast=TRUE).
summary(UD1_pHREML)$CI # home range area estimation
                             low      est     high
area (square kilometers) 7142.09 18981.64 36506.22

Step 5 - Evaluating additional biases, applying mitigation measures

summary(UD1_pHREML)$DOF["area"] # effective sample size of animal1
    area 
6.247827 
nrow(c1t) # absolute sample size
[1] 4891
UD1w_pHREML <- akde(c1t, FIT1_pHREML, weights = TRUE)
Default grid size of 2.99166666666667 hours chosen for bandwidth(...,fast=TRUE).
summary(UD1w_pHREML)$CI # home range area estimation (weighted)
                              low      est     high
area (square kilometers) 6926.422 18408.45 35403.85

Creating an extent that includes both UDs at the 95% CI level:

EXT <- extent(list(UD1_ML, UD1_pHREML, UD1w_pHREML), level = 0.95)

Plotting pHREML (with and without weights) side-by-side:

par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(c1t, UD = UD1_pHREML, ext = EXT)
DOP values missing. Assuming DOP=1.
title(expression("pHREML AKDE"["C"]))
plot(c1t, UD = UD1w_pHREML, ext = EXT)
DOP values missing. Assuming DOP=1.
title(expression("pHREML wAKDE"["C"]))
( 1 - summary(UD1_ML)$CI[1,2] / summary(UD1w_pHREML)$CI[1,2] ) * 100
[1] 11.55527
Minimum convex polygons
Model building
